title: eriogonum alatum subsp. triste
intro: Eriogonum alatum subsp. triste, also known as the Mojave Desert buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Eriogonum alatum subsp. triste can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
